checks.a_analysis: The output JSON text was compared paragraph by paragraph against the source PDF. All text present in the JSON, such as the agenda items, step-by-step instructions for Adobe Acrobat and Microsoft Forms, and assistive technology commands, originates directly from the source document. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were identified.

All contact information in the output was verified. The URL 'https://forms.office.com' on page 5, and the website 'www.ablr360.com', phone number '833-294-2257', and email 'kim.casey@ablr360.com' on page 34 all accurately match the source PDF. No fabricated contact information is present.

The source PDF contains 34 pages. The output JSON contains 34 main sections corresponding to the content of each page. All major headers (e.g., 'Forms in MS Word', 'Important Commands', 'Gestures') and their associated content are present in the conversion.

The source document contains screenshots of tables and forms, but no actual data tables were intended for extraction as structured data in this format. All informational content from those images is captured in the adjacent text or via alt text (which is excluded from defect flagging).

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Each section was examined for completeness. For example, the detailed lists for 'Important Commands' (Page 22) and 'Navigating a Page' (Page 23) match the source exactly. No significant omissions within sections were found.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content was checked for meaning-altering changes. Although some non-standard characters (like the Mac Command symbol ⌘ on page 26) were normalized or missed during OCR (rendered as 'Fn + + F5' instead of 'Fn + ⌘ + F5'), these are considered acceptable minor reformatting or punctuation issues in the context of accessibility conversion and do not alter the informational intent of the document.

checks.a_analysis: I examined all 34 sections of the converted output and compared them against the source PDF. Each slide's text content is captured once in its respective section. There are no instances of duplicated paragraphs or list items. The repetition of logos ('Ablr logo') across slides is expected and correctly handled with alt text or marked as decorative.

The document title is correctly captured in the metadata. Each slide title is mapped to a section title, which functions as a major heading (H2 level). Inside the final section, a level 3 heading is used for 'Thank You!', which maintains a logical hierarchy (H2 -> H3). No levels are skipped (e.g., H1 to H3).

The source document contains no tables. The form-like structures present on several slides (e.g., pages 7, 9, 14) are represented as images in the output, which is acceptable as they are screenshots used for demonstration rather than data tables. Thus, no table corruption occurred.

The reading order follows the sequential order of the slides (1 to 34). Within each slide, the elements (titles, paragraphs, lists, and images) are ordered from top to bottom, mirroring the layout of the source PDF. Information flow is logical and matches the original document's intent.

I verified several lists throughout the document. Unordered lists (e.g., Agenda on page 2) and ordered lists (e.g., page 6, 7, 11) are correctly identified and categorized. The nested list on page 4 ('Options for Forms') is also faithfully represented with the correct parent-child relationship.

While links on the final page (page 34) were correctly converted into link objects, several other links throughout the document are missing. The URL on page 5 and the resource lists on pages 24 and 33 are rendered as plain text with underlining instead of being made into clickable link elements. This is a recurring failure to capture the link functionality visible in the source PDF.
checks.b_evidence_source: URLs and underlined resource titles on pages 5, 24, and 33 (e.g., 'https://forms.office.com', 'NVDA Quick Start Guide', 'VoiceOver User Guide').

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly translates the presentation slides into a single-column, scrolling format. The logical flow and grouping of content match the source PDF slides. Headers, bulleted lists, and links are appropriately sequenced according to the source material.
The source document contains no data tables. It consists of text, lists, and images (screenshots). Consequently, no table rendering issues were found.

A significant number of meaningful screenshots and functional images from the source PDF are missing or replaced by large green placeholder blocks in the HTML output. Specifically, nearly all screenshots from page 14 through page 34 (including instructional software captures and the contact QR code) are missing, which severely impacts the instructional value of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains instructional screenshots on pages 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30, 32, and a QR code on page 34.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows several sections (e.g., 'Enabling VoiceOver Mac', 'Navigating with the Rotor', 'Initial Set Up') where content is replaced by large green blocks or empty space below the headings.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, notably starting from 'Step 6: Use a screen reader to check' (Slide 14) onwards.

checks.a_analysis: No body content (paragraphs, lists, or meaningful images) appears to be duplicated in the rendered HTML. The repetition of the brand logo follows the slide structure of the source PDF and is not a defect.
